subject: READINESS: P8 Slice B interaction tests
body: Torrid read-only support for P8 Slice B. Focused current test command is green with no warning: pnpm --filter @oaknational/agent-tools exec vitest run tests/collaboration-state/tui-app.unit.test.ts. Likely act-warning risk for the new work is keyboard/input or async refresh state updates emitted outside React act: wrap stdin/key emissions, updateSource emits, refresh promise resolve/reject, and the following render flush inside await act(async () => ...). Minimum tests I would land: tab focus cycles main -> agents -> queue -> directed -> main by asserting the > pane title moves; down/up scroll is bounded and cannot leave a pane looking stuck after many down presses; keyboard r shows refreshing then refreshed on success; refresh failure reports the error and preserves the prior snapshot; stale refresh rejection remains covered, ideally through the same trigger path Nebulous changes. Boundary risk: controller offsets are unbounded today while visible() clamps during render, so repeated down can make the UI feel stuck until many up presses; true bounded scroll probably needs controller knowledge of snapshot entry counts or a small clamp helper in controller, not app.tsx. Existing render helper also mutates process stdin/stdout, so use try/finally and consider a fake stdin stream to avoid leaked globals if assertions fail. Support only, not a blocker.
